title: Forty as a Beginning
subtitle: A reading on the turn into the forties as a beginning, not an ending
excerpt: A ten-minute reading on the turn into the forties. The marketing version says it is decline. The actual version is a beginning: the body settles into itself, the mind clears, the years before forty turn out to have been the apprenticeship.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The forty-as-beginning frame is shared across midlife writing, across late-life-cycle research, and across the writing of women who have lived through the decade.

practice.whenToUse: Read in the year before turning forty. Also read in the first year after, or any time the forties feel like a downgrade rather than a doorway.

content.text: The forties get a bad press. The marketing version says the body slows, the skin goes, the energy thins. The story sells creams and clinics.
The actual version is different. The forties are a beginning. The twenties were a draft. The thirties were the build. The forties are where the work starts to be made by the person you actually are.

The thirties were the decade of taking the work seriously. The career, the home, the babies if babies were coming, the partner if a partner was coming. The decade of running on the energy you had and not knowing yet which choices would hold.

At forty, you have the answers to most of the early questions. You know what you are good at. You know what you do not want any more. You know which friends are real. You know what your body can do and what is now off the table.
Why the forties feel like a beginning
The hormones shift. Most women find the shift narrows the band of patience for things that do not matter. The work that does matter gets sharper attention. The work that does not matter gets dropped.

The body changes. The change is real. It is also a re-setting: the body of the forty-year-old is built for a different kind of work than the body of the twenty-year-old, and the work it is built for is often more interesting.
The eye for what is yours sharpens. The eye for what is not yours sharpens too. The forties are when the no gets easier to say and the yes gets clearer.
What to take into the decade
Take the things you learned in your thirties about your own working hours, your own sleep, your own pace. Stop forcing the patterns that were never yours.
Take one new habit that is for the next decade, not the last one. Strength training, walking, a regular sleep window, time at the table with friends. Build it small.

Take the long view. The forties are the early years of a decade-arc that lasts until fifty. The thirty-something panic about getting old does not belong here any more. You are now in the years where the work gets good.
Original to homemade.education. The forty-as-beginning frame appears in midlife writing. It appears in late-life research. It appears in the writing of women who have lived through the decade.
